back_translation: Therefore, holy brothers, those who share in the heavenly calling, consider Jesus, the One who is the apostle and high priest of our confession
The One who was faithful to the One who appointed Him, just like Moses who was faithful in the household of God
Because Jesus is worthy of receiving more glory than Moses, just as the one who builds a house has more honor than the house itself
Because every house has a builder, but God is the Builder of all things
And Moses was faithful in the entire household of God, in the position of a trusted-servant, in order to be a witness to the things that would be spoken later
But Christ, in the position of Son, is faithful over the household of God. And we are the household of His, if we hold fast to the boldness and the boasting of the hope of ours
Therefore, as the Holy Spirit says, ‘Today, if you all hear the voice of His
Do not make the hearts of yours hard, like in the time of the rebellion, on the day of testing in the wilderness
There the ancestors of yours tested me with examination, and saw the works of mine
For forty years. Therefore I was angry with that generation, and said, “The hearts of theirs are always going astray, and they do not know the ways of mine”
Therefore I swore in the wrath of mine, “They shall not enter the rest of mine at all”’
Brothers, be careful, lest among you there is anyone who has an evil heart of unbelief, that turns away from the living God
But encourage one another every day, as long as it is still called ‘today’, so that none of you will become hardened because of the deception of sin
Because we have become those who share with Christ, if we hold fast to the original confidence firmly until the end
As the word has said, ‘Today, if you all hear the voice of His, do not make the hearts of yours hard, like in the time of the rebellion’
Because who is it that, having heard, still rebelled? Was it not all the people who came out of Egypt by way of Moses?
And with whom was God angry for forty years? Was it not those who sinned, whose corpses fell in the wilderness?
And to whom did God swear, that they should not enter the rest of His? Was it not those who disobeyed?
And we see that they were not able to enter, because of unbelief
